channel.title: Comments for page "Third Law Hub"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Third Law Hub"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I visited this page because I plan to update Russian translation of this hub, and… oh my God, I translated some tales (Unusual Investigations and some more about 3Ports), and it is truly impressive. So much versatile tales, small canons, canons inside those canons, connectivity with Those Twisted Pines and 3Ports… I know what I will do during the next n∈[2; 5], n∈$\mathbb{N}$ years! There's so many tales to read and translate, and I hope they all are interesting and captivating!

P.S. You probably need to delete table with translations, because now interwiki module is working.

item.title: Re: +150
item.content:encoded: Job well done, GW!

<GW> .sea —tags third-law —summary
<jarvis> Found 58 pages by 12 authors. They have a total rating of +4666, with an average of +80. The pages were created between 3 years ago and 5 months ago. The highest rated page is SCP-2308: Futures Trading at +248.

Third Law is currently the third largest canon on the site (Behind Those Twisted Pines and Dread & Circuses) and has the third highest total rating (after Those Twisted Pines and Resurrection). That it is composed almost entirely of tales and GOI Formats and still manages to pull an average rating of +80 is honestly pretty fantastic. Thanks to everyone who has read or contributed, and be sure to keep your eyes on this hub— we've got some big plans for the future of this canon.

item.title: Re: Error in history tab
item.content:encoded: *blushes*. Thanks for that gentle reminder. ^.^

item.title: Re: Error in history tab
item.content:encoded: Working, in this case, is a noun. As in, like, a magical working. They did a ritual which killed god.

item.title: Error in history tab
item.content:encoded:
History tab says

when a cabal of German occultists completed a working which resulted in

a working what?!

[Good hub. Good canons. Thank you all authors. :) ]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey hey, another update. How snazzy.

In all seriousness, this is quite a nice addition of content. The timelines alone are quite immersive, and the list of continuity makes this canon seem quite large and far-reaching, dipping in and out of various bits of Foundation lore to make a very open setting.

Alas, I already have upvoted.

item.title: "welcome to third law, the very first eigencanon"
item.content:encoded:
So this is an absolutely massive update to the hub, which has taken over three months to fully assemble. Pretty much every part of the hub has been overhauled or rewritten, so the list of changes is "everything". This should hopefully make the canon a lot more accessible for new readers, since one of the things I hear pretty often is a concern about where to start with this canon. (The answer is anywhere).

Thanks to my fellow contributors, TyGently, ARD, Taffeta, and NineVolt for assistance at various points in drafting and editing this update, as well as to Conwell and tawny for additional critique and input.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded:
Wow. That is especially impressive given how much of this canon is GoI formats and tales. Only 9/47 are SCP articles.

I think this is the most successful collaborative project I have ever been involved in, in any media, both in terms of relative success but also in the level of creative sympatico among contributors.

I am extremely proud to be in this particular fellowship.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded:
6:15 AM <@Taffeta> .s -t third-law —summary
6:16 AM <%jarvis> taffeta: Found 47 pages by 9 authors. They have a total rating of +3070, with an average of +65. The pages were created between 2 years ago and 24 days ago. The highest rated page is SCP-2308: Futures Trading at +211.

in awe at the size of this lad, absolute unit
A big step forward for the canon! Here's to 200!

item.title: Re: Why doesnt anyone mention TCAE?
item.content:encoded: TCAE plays a heavy role in SCP-2176 and is mentioned by name several times in said article.

item.title: Why doesnt anyone mention TCAE?
item.content:encoded:
Titan Consumer Appliances and Electronics was never mentioned, and this kinda makes me feel if it was randomly edited into the canon. Help!

Thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: holy gosh, you've made a gigantic canon here

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I blurbed my works, FWIW.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Yet another update, this one a bit more substantial. Changes enumerated below:

Yeah, this is a long list
Told ya so
  • Added skips and goi formats to the home tab
  • Added blurbs to all articles on the home tab
  • Folded the Successors tab into the Prometheus Labs tab
  • Retitled the Paratech Trade tab to Other Organizations
  • Cut the publications list from the References tab
  • Added a list of major characters to the References tab
  • Massively expanded the glossary
  • Moved all the separate lists of paratech to a single list in the References tab
  • Pruned the locations list
  • Removed Wondertainment from the GOI list for the time being
  • Added ICSUT and Black Queen to the GOI list
  • Reorganized the GOI list a little bit
  • Added a bit of CSS to increase the spacing of lists
  • Updated the Contributing tab to reflect these changes
  • Removed the complete list of stuffs from the contributing tab
  • Expanded the suggested reading a bit
  • Probably other stuff I forgot

Thanks to Conwell, A Random Day, TyGently, and Aiden Eldritch for their assistance in getting the update done.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: A minor addendum, since a semi-major update has been made. There are now links to the canon IRC chatroom (#Apotheosis) and brainstorming sandbox in the contributing tab, as well as a module to display a complete listing of all works within this canon (as it is mainly tales that are linked on the main tab, and not all goi formats and skips are linked in the info tabs). This list is not in chronological order, and is instead sorted by rating. Additionally, blurbs for three GOIs were added to the paratech trade tab last month, something which went unannounced at the time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Prometheus Labs science sneers at mere set paradoxes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is done, in accordance with the prophecy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes. I was leaving tagging it up to you, just to make sure you wanted to include your stuff in the canon. (And if you've changed your mind, that's completely fine, I'll make sure to edit the page accordingly.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Only if you want it to. GreenWolf, TyGently, and I had agreed before posting this hub to tag our parts of Apotheosis as third-law.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Should my stuff here have the third-law tag? It currently doesn't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this means that I need to write more about Bill Pastor and Choronzon Consulting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have no idea what's going on, all I know is it is blue and thus worthy of an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does the set of all sets not contain itself?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's probably some kind of set paradox.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, my original implication was that this made the Prometheus Hub look dry in comparison when respect about the information of said. There's a great ark-building here for the Prometheus Labs GoI and would like to see more stories around ideas from here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are things inside this canon that are outside this canon…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's nothing stopping you from using things from this canon outside of Third Law. Many people have used the magic system that Clef outlined for the GOC canon, but few of those articles are actually in the GOC canon. Heck, people have already used things from Third Law outside of Third Law, like Conwell's use of Three Portlands in Austringers. In fact, I actively encourage people to do this kind of stuff, as it leads to all sorts of wonderful and creative stories that wouldn't or couldn't exist within the confines of Third Law.

TL;DR: Third Law isn't some kind of exclusive clubhouse for these ideas or characters, feel free to use Third Law stuff outside of Third Law.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In the hub's defense, it had to be created over just one month by a single person. This canon is the result of more than half a year's work by five skilled writers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The various detailed info of Prometheus subsidiaries, successors and relationships with other GoIs makes this hub outshine completely the standard Prometheus Labs hub, but I guess since this is a canon, not all of this should be considered for main-universe Prometheus, I guess…

BTW, this is all nice work, good job GreenWolf!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Having seen this hub in various stages of development, let me just take the time to say that this is quite an accomplishment. The theme is great and the content of the hub has some solid world building. You can tell a lot of blood, sweat, and tears went into making this.

Good job Greenwolf, TyGently, ARD, and AE. Here's hoping your band of merry writers grows.

+1

item.title: guy who wrote that last tale post
item.content:encoded:
fuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuture

In all seriousness, I'm inordinately proud that y'all managed to get this far with this canon and I'm just as proud to have been part of the process. Good work Team Apotheosis Third Law.

ETA: And I swear to god the next person to ask me "psat setanta when" is going to get kicked into next Wednesday, because that's when I'm planning to drop it.

item.title: vice-author post?
item.content:encoded:
We did it.

It took seven months, but we (read: GreenWolf) made the pipe dream of a PL canon a reality.

Many, many, many thanks to everybody along the way who critiqued, brainstormed, wrote, and voted on all the tales, SCPs, and GOI Formats that inhabit this brave new world.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm proud to have been a part of this, in the sense that I'm proud to have been present in the team IRC room for the MTF contest when the real masterminds were figuring it out.

I feel like I contributed.

EDIT:

+ Obligatory
- Obligatory

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This theme is dope.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
And here we are. Finally. If you'll excuse me, I'm going to go pass out now. Will write more later.

Edit:

It's somewhat ironic that, as a nominally successful author on this site, I increasingly find myself at a loss for words. Perhaps writing for the site is sapping my "talent", a la the classic 1996 film Space Jam… nope, I still can't say that with a straight face.

I suppose I should start with the beginning. Rather obvious starting point in hindsight, wouldn't you say?

+ A 'Brief' History Lesson
- TL;DR People wrote things, I was one of them

The seeds for Third Law were planted as far back as 2014, with the Groups of Interest Contest. I did not participate in this contest1, but A Random Day — the man who would become my collaborator and partner in creating this canon — did. He was part of the ill-fated Prometheus Labs team that ended up not placing in the contest after most of its members bailed. However, the PL team — which at this point was essentially just ARD — did give us the PL GOI format.

Fast forward a little bit more than a year, and we find rookie author GreenWolf — that's me, in case you haven't been paying attention — coming off the high of his first massively successful article2 and eagerly looking for the next big thing to write. This is one of those moments that Terry Pratchett referred to as the trousers of time — a point in history where the result of a single decision makes all the difference. I asked TwistedGears what I should do for my next article, and he told me to write something about Prometheus Labs. Intrigued, I spent the afternoon going over what PL articles currently existed on the site, which is how I discovered the PL GOI format. I was instantly won over by the format, and set about writing my own. Then I wrote another. Then I wrote a terrible tale based off of the second GOI format that no one can prove ever existed.

By this point, I was completely and absolutely in love with Prometheus Labs as a GOI, and was determined to revitalize them. I wanted to change the site's perception of them as a group of failures who had every project go horribly wrong, instead depicting them as researchers working on the cutting edge of science — both anomalous and mundane — with all of the risks and rewards that entails. In retrospect, such an endeavor was almost certainly too ambitious, especially for a rookie writer. And yet, here we are.

When the MTF contest rolled around, I immediately teamed up with ARD with the intent of doing an MTF that focused on Prometheus Labs and the Foundation's interaction with them. We added sirpudding and TyGently to our ranks. It was this team that was responsible for laying the foundations for what would become Third Law, and it was during our planning meetings that Third Law first became something more substantial than a collection of vaguely formed ideas inside my head.

Over the following months, Third Law would both grow and change, expanding in focus and scope from just a PL canon to a canon about paratech, viewed through the lens of PL. I setup a proto-hub for the canon on the sandbox site, which would gradually grow over the following months as more details of the setting were fleshed out and more articles were written for Third Law. And it will hopefully continue to grow as more authors write articles for Third Law.

So, with the history lesson out of the way, I suppose I should answer the inevitable question: What exactly is this canon for?

+ What is this?
- 'This' is a pronoun.

First, let me tell you what Third Law is not.

  • It is not a canon dedicated exclusively to Prometheus Labs.
  • It is not a canon dedicated exclusively to Apotheosis.
  • It is not a canon dedicated to the color blue.
  • It is not a pipe.

Third Law is a canon focusing on paratech — on its development, its regulation, its use, its effects, and on the people who do or are involved in these things. It takes as its fundamental axioms the fact that the anomalous can be scientifically studied and eventually understood, and that this parascience drives a thriving industry and market just behind the Veil. Naturally, this canon will have a strong focus on Prometheus Labs, as they are/were the single largest manufacturer of paratech, but it is not solely about them. In fact, at the time of this writing, PL only directly appears in two of the tales for this canon.

TL;DR: This is a canon dedicated to magitech and science fantasy (in the sense that the fantastical elements can be studied as a science). It assumes a large anomalous world, a weak but still intact Veil, and the strength of the scientific method in the face of the unknown and incomprehensible.

Finally, I'd like to extend my thanks and appreciation to everyone who helped make this canon possible:

  • A Random Day, without whom there would be no Third Law, nor an abundance of all caps GOI format articles. You've been an essential part of the development of this canon since before its conception, and I'm glad to have had the opportunity to collaborate with you.
  • TyGently, don't discount the importance of your contributions. You've been a constant and valuable source of ideas, criticism, and support for me and for this canon.
  • sirpudding, whose input during the MTF contest helped shape the fundamental nature of this canon. Without you and your ideas, both Apotheosis and Third Law wouldn't be nearly as good as they are now.
  • Aiden Eldritch, the individual who finally pushed this into official canon status with an excellent cyberpunk tale reminiscent of Snow Crash. Your support and contributions are no less essential for having come later, and I'm glad to have you on board. I look forward to seeing you post Operation Llewyn Dark.
  • TwistedGears, who provided the initial impetus that led to me writing PL stuff, and eventually Third Law, and who's criticism during the early stages of the hub planning and world-building pushed this away from being an exclusively PL canon, a change in scope which has only been an improvement.
  • Randomini, a bountiful source of inspiration, criticism, and technical help with the finer aspect of CSS.
  • Conwell, who I believe has helped critique every tale written for this canon so far. I'd also like to thank you for providing the site with Anderson Robotics, a GOI that has been an amazing addition to the wiki in general, and a source of some amazing stories for this canon.
  • Dr Kens, for assistance with some of the technical and aesthetic elements of the CSS theme for this. Also for that kicking rad mock movie poster for Apotheosis you made during the MTF contest.
  • minmin, your criticism was invaluable to the tales in this canon.
  • Jekeled, your crit was incredibly helpful too.
  • Decibelle, for your support and suggestions.
  • And to everyone who helped critique a tale or skip or goi-format for Third Law. If your name isn't up there, it's only because I'm terrible at remembering names.

Scientia Potentia Est

Footnotes
1. Although I was a member of the site at the time, I had drifted away and would not return until the following year, and wouldn't author a successful article until that November.
2. SCP-2206 - Maximum League Baseball for those keeping score at home.
